description: A psychology professor needs individual angular rotation slopes computed for a mental rotation experiment class demonstration using the dataset at ~/pebl/data/mental_rotation_data.csv. For each participant, compute the mental rotation slope (ms per degree), intercept, and r-squared by fitting a linear regression of mean reaction times on angle (0, 45, 90, 135, 180) using only correct "same" trials. Additionally, calculate the overall accuracy across all trial types for each participant. Exclude participant s99 from the group-level statistics due to a response box malfunction. Produce a JSON report at ~/pebl/analysis/mental_rotation_report.json containing a list of participants with their individual metrics (id, slope_ms_per_deg, intercept_ms, r_squared, mean_accuracy) or exclusion status (excluded: true, reason) for s99, along with group-level metrics (group_mean_slope_ms_per_deg, group_sd_slope_ms_per_deg, and group_mean_accuracy) for the valid participants.

metadata.data_source: PEBL Battery Normative Data (Simulated Empirical Ex-Gaussian)
